req.title: Announcement rollout — variant 4
req.statement: A system-admin shall publish an org-wide announcement to employees in chosen departments; publishing without any department selection shall be rejected. Additionally, the operation must be rate-limited per user.

tests.name: naive_case
tests.executable:
```python
"""
Pytest tests for org-wide announcement publishing feature.

Requirements covered:
1. System-admin can publish announcements to chosen departments.
2. Publishing without any department selection is rejected.
3. The operation is rate-limited per user.
"""

import time
from datetime import datetime, timedelta
from unittest.mock import MagicMock, patch, call
from http import HTTPStatus

import pytest

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Fake domain objects / helpers (replace with real imports in your codebase)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class ValidationError(Exception):
    pass


class PermissionError(Exception):
    pass


class RateLimitExceededError(Exception):
    pass


class AnnouncementService:
    """
    Thin wrapper around the real service – stands in for the system under test.
    Replace the import with your actual module path.
    """

    MAX_REQUESTS_PER_WINDOW = 5          # max publishes per user per window
    RATE_LIMIT_WINDOW_SECONDS = 60       # rolling window in seconds

    def __init__(self, announcement_repo, department_repo, rate_limiter, auth_service):
        self.announcement_repo = announcement_repo
        self.department_repo   = department_repo
        self.rate_limiter      = rate_limiter
        self.auth_service      = auth_service

    def publish(self, actor_id: str, title: str, body: str, department_ids: list[str]) -> dict:
        # 1. Authorization
        if not self.auth_service.is_system_admin(actor_id):
            raise PermissionError(f"User {actor_id!r} is not a system-admin.")

        # 2. Department validation
        if not department_ids:
            raise ValidationError("At least one department must be selected.")

        unknown = [d for d in department_ids if not self.department_repo.exists(d)]
        if unknown:
            raise ValidationError(f"Unknown department(s): {unknown}")

        # 3. Rate limiting
        allowed = self.rate_limiter.check_and_increment(
            actor_id,
            limit=self.MAX_REQUESTS_PER_WINDOW,
            window_seconds=self.RATE_LIMIT_WINDOW_SECONDS,
        )
        if not allowed:
            raise RateLimitExceededError(
                f"User {actor_id!r} has exceeded the publish rate limit."
            )

        # 4. Persist & return
        announcement = self.announcement_repo.create(
            author_id=actor_id,
            title=title,
            body=body,
            department_ids=department_ids,
            published_at=datetime.utcnow(),
        )
        return announcement


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Fixtures
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

@pytest.fixture()
def mock_announcement_repo():
    repo = MagicMock()
    repo.create.return_value = {
        "id": "ann-001",
        "title": "Test Announcement",
        "body": "Hello org!",
        "department_ids": ["dept-eng", "dept-hr"],
        "published_at": datetime(2024, 1, 1, 12, 0, 0),
    }
    return repo


@pytest.fixture()
def mock_department_repo():
    repo = MagicMock()
    # By default every department lookup succeeds
    repo.exists.return_value = True
    return repo


@pytest.fixture()
def mock_rate_limiter():
    limiter = MagicMock()
    # By default rate-limit is not exceeded
    limiter.check_and_increment.return_value = True
    return limiter


@pytest.fixture()
def mock_auth_service():
    auth = MagicMock()
    # By default the actor IS a system-admin
    auth.is_system_admin.return_value = True
    return auth


@pytest.fixture()
def service(mock_announcement_repo, mock_department_repo, mock_rate_limiter, mock_auth_service):
    return AnnouncementService(
        announcement_repo=mock_announcement_repo,
        department_repo=mock_department_repo,
        rate_limiter=mock_rate_limiter,
        auth_service=mock_auth_service,
    )


SYSTEM_ADMIN_ID = "user-admin-001"
REGULAR_USER_ID = "user-employee-042"

VALID_PAYLOAD = dict(
    title="Q1 All-Hands Summary",
    body="Please review the attached slides.",
    department_ids=["dept-eng", "dept-hr"],
)


# ===========================================================================
# 1. Happy-path: system-admin publishes to one or more departments
# ===========================================================================

class TestPublishSuccess:

    def test_publish_returns_announcement_dict(self, service, mock_announcement_repo):
        result = service.publish(SYSTEM_ADMIN_ID, **VALID_PAYLOAD)

        assert isinstance(result, dict)
        assert result["id"] == "ann-001"

    def test_publish_stores_correct_author(self, service, mock_announcement_repo):
        service.publish(SYSTEM_ADMIN_ID, **VALID_PAYLOAD)

        mock_announcement_repo.create.assert_called_once()
        kwargs = mock_announcement_repo.create.call_args.kwargs
        assert kwargs["author_id"] == SYSTEM_ADMIN_ID

    def test_publish_stores_correct_departments(self, service, mock_announcement_repo):
        service.publish(SYSTEM_ADMIN_ID, **VALID_PAYLOAD)

        kwargs = mock_announcement_repo.create.call_args.kwargs
        assert set(kwargs["department_ids"]) == {"dept-eng", "dept-hr"}

    def test_publish_stores_title_and_body(self, service, mock_announcement_repo):
        service.publish(SYSTEM_ADMIN_ID, **VALID_PAYLOAD)

        kwargs = mock_announcement_repo.create.call_args.kwargs
        assert kwargs["title"] == VALID_PAYLOAD["title"]
        assert kwargs["body"]  == VALID_PAYLOAD["body"]

    def test_publish_stores_published_at_timestamp(self, service, mock_announcement_repo):
        before = datetime.utcnow()
        service.publish(SYSTEM_ADMIN_ID, **VALID_PAYLOAD)
        after = datetime.utcnow()

        kwargs = mock_announcement_repo.create.call_args.kwargs
        assert before <= kwargs["published_at"] <= after

    def test_publish_to_single_department_is_accepted(self, service, mock_announcement_repo):
        payload = {**VALID_PAYLOAD, "department_ids": ["dept-eng"]}
        result = service.publish(SYSTEM_ADMIN_ID, **payload)

        assert result is not None
        mock_announcement_repo.create.assert_called_once()

    def test_publish_to_many_departments_is_accepted(self, service, mock_announcement_repo):
        many_depts = [f"dept-{i:03d}" for i in range(20)]
        payload = {**VALID_PAYLOAD, "department_ids": many_depts}
        service.publish(SYSTEM_ADMIN_ID, **payload)

        kwargs = mock_announcement_repo.create.call_args.kwargs
        assert len(kwargs["department_ids"]) == 20

    def test_department_existence_is_verified_for_each_id(
        self, service, mock_department_repo
    ):
        service.publish(SYSTEM_ADMIN_ID, **VALID_PAYLOAD)

        expected_calls = [call("dept-eng"), call("dept-hr")]
        mock_department_repo.exists.assert_has_calls(expected_calls, any_order=True)

    def test_rate_limiter_is_called_with_correct_actor(
        self, service, mock_rate_limiter
    ):
        service.publish(SYSTEM_ADMIN_ID, **VALID_PAYLOAD)

        mock_rate_limiter.check_and_increment.assert_called_once()
        args, kwargs = mock_rate_limiter.check_and_increment.call_args
        actor = args[0] if args else kwargs.get("actor_id") or kwargs.get("user_id")
        assert actor == SYSTEM_ADMIN_ID


# ===========================================================================
# 2. Department selection validation
# ===========================================================================

class TestDepartmentValidation:

    def test_empty_department_list_raises_validation_error(self, service):
        with pytest.raises(ValidationError, match="department"):
            service.publish(SYSTEM_ADMIN_ID, title="T", body="B", department_ids=[])

    def test_none_department_list_raises_validation_error(self, service):
        with pytest.raises(ValidationError, match="department"):
            service.publish(SYSTEM_ADMIN_ID, title="T", body="B", department_ids=None)

    def test_empty_list_does_not_call_repo(self, service, mock_announcement_repo):
        with pytest.raises(ValidationError):
            service.publish(SYSTEM_ADMIN_ID, title="T", body="B", department_ids=[])

        mock_announcement_repo.create.assert_not_called()

    def test_unknown_department_id_raises_validation_error(
        self, service, mock_department_repo
    ):
        mock_department_repo.exists.side_effect = lambda d: d != "dept-unknown"

        with pytest.raises(ValidationError, match="dept-unknown"):
            service.publish(
                SYSTEM_ADMIN_ID,
                title="T",
                body="B",
                department_ids=["dept-eng", "dept-unknown"],
            )

    def test_unknown_department_does_not_create_announcement(
        self, service, mock_department_repo, mock_announcement_repo
    ):
        mock_department_repo.exists.return_value = False

        with pytest.raises(ValidationError):
            service.publish(SYSTEM_ADMIN_ID, **VALID_PAYLOAD)

        mock_announcement_repo.create.assert_not_called()

    def test_all_unknown_departments_raises_validation_error(
        self, service, mock_department_repo
    ):
        mock_department_repo.exists.return_value = False

        with pytest.raises(ValidationError):
            service.publish(SYSTEM_ADMIN_ID, **VALID_PAYLOAD)

    @pytest.mark.parametrize("bad_value", [None, [], ""])
    def test_various_falsy_department_inputs_are_rejected(self, service, bad_value):
        with pytest.raises((ValidationError, TypeError)):
            service.publish(SYSTEM_ADMIN_ID, title="T", body="B", department_ids=bad_value)


# ===========================================================================
# 3. Authorization: only system-admins may publish
# ===========================================================================

class TestAuthorization:

    def test_non_admin_cannot_publish(self, service, mock_auth_service):
        mock_auth_service.is_system_admin.return_value = False

        with pytest.raises(PermissionError):
            service.publish(REGULAR_USER_ID, **VALID_PAYLOAD)

    def test_non_admin_publish_does_not_create_announcement(
        self, service, mock_auth_service, mock_announcement_repo
    ):
        mock_auth_service.is_system_admin.return_value = False

        with pytest.raises(PermissionError):
            service.publish(REGULAR_USER_ID, **VALID_PAYLOAD)

        mock_announcement_repo.create.assert_not_called()

    def test_non_admin_is_checked_before_rate_limiter(
        self, service, mock_auth_service, mock_rate_limiter
    ):
        """Authorization must be evaluated before consuming rate-limit quota."""
        mock_auth_service.is_system_admin.return_value = False

        with pytest.raises(PermissionError):
            service.publish(REGULAR_USER_ID, **VALID_PAYLOAD)

        mock_rate_limiter.check_and_increment.assert_not_called()

    def test_admin_identity_is_verified(self, service, mock_auth_service):
        service.publish(SYSTEM_ADMIN_ID, **VALID_PAYLOAD)

        mock_auth_service.is_system_admin.assert_called_once_with(SYSTEM_ADMIN_ID)


# ===========================================================================
# 4. Rate limiting
# ===========================================================================

class TestRateLimiting:

    def test_rate_limit_exceeded_raises_rate_limit_error(
        self, service, mock_rate_limiter
    ):
        mock_rate_limiter.check_and_increment.return_value = False

        with pytest.raises(RateLimitExceededError):
            service.publish(SYSTEM_ADMIN_ID, **VALID_PAYLOAD)

    def test_rate_limited_request_does_not_create_announcement(
        self, service, mock_rate_limiter, mock_announcement_repo
    ):
        mock_rate_limiter.check_and_increment.return_value = False

        with pytest.raises(RateLimitExceededError):
            service.publish(SYSTEM_ADMIN_ID, **VALID_PAYLOAD)

        mock_announcement_repo.create.assert_not_called()

    def test_rate_limit_window_passed_to_limiter(self, service, mock_rate_limiter):
        service.publish(SYSTEM_ADMIN_ID, **VALID_PAYLOAD)

        _, kwargs = mock_rate_limiter.check_and_increment.call_args
        assert kwargs.get("window_seconds") == AnnouncementService.RATE_LIMIT_WINDOW_SECONDS

    def test_rate_limit_max_requests_passed_to_limiter(self, service, mock_rate_limiter):
        service.publish(SYSTEM_ADMIN_ID, **VALID_PAYLOAD)

        _, kwargs = mock_rate_limiter.check_and_increment.call_args
        assert kwargs.get("limit") == AnnouncementService.MAX_REQUESTS_PER_WINDOW

    def test_rate_limit_is_per_user_not_global(
        self, service, mock_rate_limiter, mock_auth_service
    ):
        """
        Two different admins should each get their own rate-limit check,
        not share the same counter.
        """
        mock_auth_service.is_system_admin.return_value = True
        mock_rate_limiter.check_and_increment.return_value = True

        admin_a = "admin-aaa"
        admin_b = "admin-bbb"

        service.publish(admin_a, **VALID_PAYLOAD)
        service.publish(admin_b, **VALID_PAYLOAD)

        calls = mock_rate_limiter.check_and_increment.call_args_list
        actors = [c.args[0] for c in calls]

        assert admin_a in actors
        assert admin_b in actors

    def test_successive_allowed_publishes_each_check_rate_limit(
        self, service, mock_rate_limiter
    ):
        limit = AnnouncementService.MAX_REQUESTS_PER_WINDOW
        for _ in range(limit):
            service.publish(SYSTEM_ADMIN_ID, **VALID_PAYLOAD)

        assert mock_rate_limiter.check_and_increment.call_count == limit

    def test_publish_fails_on_exactly_limit_plus_one_attempt(
        self, service, mock_rate_limiter
    ):
        limit = AnnouncementService.MAX_REQUESTS_PER_WINDOW
        # Allow exactly `limit` calls, then deny
        mock_rate_limiter.check_and_increment.side_effect = (
            [True] * limit + [False]
        )

        for _ in range(limit):
            service.publish(SYSTEM_ADMIN_ID, **VALID_PAYLOAD)

        with pytest.raises(RateLimitExceededError):
            service.publish(SYSTEM_ADMIN_ID, **VALID_PAYLOAD)

    def test_rate_limit_error_message_identifies_user(
        self, service, mock_rate_limiter
    ):
        mock_rate_limiter.check_and_increment.return_value = False

        with pytest.raises(RateLimitExceededError, match=SYSTEM_ADMIN_ID):
            service.publish(SYSTEM_ADMIN_ID, **VALID_PAYLOAD)


# ===========================================================================
# 5. Interaction / ordering guards
# ===========================================================================

class TestOperationOrdering:
    """
    Guards against accidental side-effects when early validations fail.
    """

    def test_validation_error_prevents_rate_limit_consumption(
        self, service, mock_rate_limiter
    ):
        with pytest.raises(ValidationError):
            service.publish(SYSTEM_ADMIN_ID, title="T", body="B", department_ids=[])

        mock_rate_limiter.check_and_increment.assert_not_called()

    def test_validation_error_prevents_repo_write(
        self, service, mock_announcement_repo
    ):
        with pytest.raises(ValidationError):
            service.publish(SYSTEM_ADMIN_ID, title="T", body="B", department_ids=[])

        mock_announcement_repo.create.assert_not_called()

    def test_permission_error_prevents_repo_write(
        self, service, mock_auth_service, mock_announcement_repo
    ):
        mock_auth_service.is_system_admin.return_value = False

        with pytest.raises(PermissionError):
            service.publish(REGULAR_USER_ID, **VALID_PAYLOAD)

        mock_announcement_repo.create.assert_not_called()

    def test_rate_limit_error_prevents_repo_write(
        self, service, mock_rate_limiter, mock_announcement_repo
    ):
        mock_rate_limiter.check_and_increment.return_value = False

        with pytest.raises(RateLimitExceededError):
            service.publish(SYSTEM_ADMIN_ID, **VALID_PAYLOAD)

        mock_announcement_repo.create.assert_not_called()


# ===========================================================================
# 6. Edge-case / boundary tests
# ===========================================================================

class TestEdgeCases:

    def test_publish_with_empty_title_is_handled(self, service):
        """Domain may or may not accept empty titles; ensure no unhandled crash."""
        try:
            service.publish(
                SYSTEM_ADMIN_ID, title="", body="Body", department_ids=["dept-eng"]
            )
        except (ValidationError, ValueError):
            pass  # Either raising a clear error or succeeding is acceptable

    def test_publish_with_very_long_body_does_not_raise_unexpected_error(
        self, service
    ):
        long_body = "x" * 100_000
        result = service.publish(
            SYSTEM_ADMIN_ID, title="T", body=long_body, department_ids=["dept-eng"]
        )
        assert result is not None

    def test_duplicate_department_ids_are_forwarded(
        self, service, mock_announcement_repo
    ):
        payload = {**VALID_PAYLOAD, "department_ids": ["dept-eng", "dept-eng"]}
        service.publish(SYSTEM_ADMIN_ID, **payload)

        kwargs = mock_announcement_repo.create.call_args.kwargs
        # The service should handle or forward duplicates without error
        assert "dept-eng" in kwargs["department_ids"]

    @pytest.mark.parametrize("departments", [
        ["dept-a"],
        ["dept-a", "dept-b"],
        ["dept-a", "dept-b", "dept-c", "dept-d", "dept-e"],
    ])
    def test_various_valid_department_counts_succeed(self, service, departments):
        result = service.publish(
            SYSTEM_ADMIN_ID, title="T", body="B", department_ids=departments
        )
        assert result is not None
```

---

## What the tests cover and why

| Test class | Requirement mapped |
|---|---|
| `TestPublishSuccess` | Admin can publish; correct data is persisted; each department id is validated |
| `TestDepartmentValidation` | Empty / `None` / unknown departments are **rejected** with `ValidationError` |
| `TestAuthorization` | Only `system-admin` role may publish; non-admins get `PermissionError` before any side-effect occurs |
| `TestRateLimiting` | Rate-limit is checked **per user**; exceeding it raises `RateLimitExceededError`; the correct `limit` and `window_seconds` are forwarded to the limiter |
| `TestOperationOrdering` | Early-exit guard: a failure in auth, validation, or rate-limiting **must not** reach the repo write |
| `TestEdgeCases` | Boundary / stress inputs (empty title, huge body, duplicate dept IDs, parametrised dept counts) |

Key design decisions:
- **All external dependencies are mocked** – the tests are pure unit tests with no I/O.
- **Rate-limit logic lives in a `RateLimiter` collaborator** – the service only calls it; integration tests for the limiter itself would live in a separate file.
- **Ordering assertions** verify that quota is not consumed when earlier validations fail, preventing waste of user quota on bad requests.

```python
"""
Pytest tests for org-wide announcement publishing feature.

Requirements covered:
1. System-admin can publish announcements to chosen departments.
2. Publishing without any department selection is rejected.
3. The operation is rate-limited per user.
"""

import time
from datetime import datetime, timedelta
from unittest.mock import MagicMock, patch, call
from http import HTTPStatus

import pytest

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Fake domain objects / helpers (replace with real imports in your codebase)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class ValidationError(Exception):
    pass


class PermissionError(Exception):
    pass


class RateLimitExceededError(Exception):
    pass


class AnnouncementService:
    """
    Thin wrapper around the real service – stands in for the system under test.
    Replace the import with your actual module path.
    """

    MAX_REQUESTS_PER_WINDOW = 5          # max publishes per user per window
    RATE_LIMIT_WINDOW_SECONDS = 60       # rolling window in seconds

    def __init__(self, announcement_repo, department_repo, rate_limiter, auth_service):
        self.announcement_repo = announcement_repo
        self.department_repo   = department_repo
        self.rate_limiter      = rate_limiter
        self.auth_service      = auth_service

    def publish(self, actor_id: str, title: str, body: str, department_ids: list[str]) -> dict:
        # 1. Authorization
        if not self.auth_service.is_system_admin(actor_id):
            raise PermissionError(f"User {actor_id!r} is not a system-admin.")

        # 2. Department validation
        if not department_ids:
            raise ValidationError("At least one department must be selected.")

        unknown = [d for d in department_ids if not self.department_repo.exists(d)]
        if unknown:
            raise ValidationError(f"Unknown department(s): {unknown}")

        # 3. Rate limiting
        allowed = self.rate_limiter.check_and_increment(
            actor_id,
            limit=self.MAX_REQUESTS_PER_WINDOW,
            window_seconds=self.RATE_LIMIT_WINDOW_SECONDS,
        )
        if not allowed:
            raise RateLimitExceededError(
                f"User {actor_id!r} has exceeded the publish rate limit."
            )

        # 4. Persist & return
        announcement = self.announcement_repo.create(
            author_id=actor_id,
            title=title,
            body=body,
            department_ids=department_ids,
            published_at=datetime.utcnow(),
        )
        return announcement


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Fixtures
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

@pytest.fixture()
def mock_announcement_repo():
    repo = MagicMock()
    repo.create.return_value = {
        "id": "ann-001",
        "title": "Test Announcement",
        "body": "Hello org!",
        "department_ids": ["dept-eng", "dept-hr"],
        "published_at": datetime(2024, 1, 1, 12, 0, 0),
    }
    return repo


@pytest.fixture()
def mock_department_repo():
    repo = MagicMock()
    # By default every department lookup succeeds
    repo.exists.return_value = True
    return repo


@pytest.fixture()
def mock_rate_limiter():
    limiter = MagicMock()
    # By default rate-limit is not exceeded
    limiter.check_and_increment.return_value = True
    return limiter


@pytest.fixture()
def mock_auth_service():
    auth = MagicMock()
    # By default the actor IS a system-admin
    auth.is_system_admin.return_value = True
    return auth


@pytest.fixture()
def service(mock_announcement_repo, mock_department_repo, mock_rate_limiter, mock_auth_service):
    return AnnouncementService(
        announcement_repo=mock_announcement_repo,
        department_repo=mock_department_repo,
        rate_limiter=mock_rate_limiter,
        auth_service=mock_auth_service
